# hyperfluid_backup_target (Resource)

An external (S3-compatible) backup target. Credentials are referenced as secret names, never inline.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "hyperfluid_env" "default" {
  name = "default"
}

# Credentials are referenced by secret name, never inline.
resource "hyperfluid_backup_target" "offsite" {
  env                        = data.hyperfluid_env.default.id
  name                          = "offsite"
  endpoint_url                  = "https://s3.eu-west-3.amazonaws.com"
  destination_path              = "s3://my-backups/postgres/"
  access_key_secret_name        = "aws-backup-access-key"
  secret_access_key_secret_name = "aws-backup-secret-key"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `access_key_secret_name` (String) Name of the secret holding the S3 access key id.
- `destination_path` (String) Bucket + prefix, e.g. s3://backups/.
- `endpoint_url` (String) S3-compatible endpoint URL, e.g. https://s3.eu-west-3.amazonaws.com.
- `env` (String) Environment id the target belongs to. Changing this forces a new target.
- `name` (String) Backup target name (slug). Changing this forces a new target.
- `secret_access_key_secret_name` (String) Name of the secret holding the S3 secret access key.

### Optional

- `description` (String) Free-form description.
- `insecure` (Boolean) Skip TLS verification when probing the endpoint (dev only). Changing this forces a new target.
- `tags` (List of String) User-defined tags.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `phase` (String) Current lifecycle phase.
- `slug` (String) Derived slug.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Backup target import id is the backup target id (uuid).
terraform import hyperfluid_backup_target.offsite 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000
```
